title: Capital Structure: If a company has 400000 in equity and its stock...
summary: Analyze a concept in theories of finance related to Capital Structure.
prompt: If a company has $400,000 in equity and its stock has a required return of 12%, what is the value of the firm if it has a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.5?
solution: Using WACC: WACC = (E/V)rE + (D/V)rD(1-T) => V = D + E; hence, if D/E = 0.5, then V = $400,000 + $200,000 = $600,000.
